subject: [PATCH 3/8] mm/zsmalloc: Introduce objcgs pointer in struct zpdesc

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Reviewer Shakeel Butt suggested replacing the objcgs pointer type from unsigned long to struct obj_cgroup **, citing that this would eliminate the need for pointer conversions during storage and access.
reviews.raw_body:
Why not just strore struct obj_cgroup ** instead of unsigned long? You will not
need to do conversions when storing or accessing.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that casting to unsigned long and then back to obj_cgroup pointer is unnecessary, agreed to change it to obj_cgroup ** in the next version.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, that makes sense to me : -)
I guess if we're going to be accessing it with the zpdesc_objcgs and
zpdesc_set_objcgs helpers anyways, we can abstract away the casting to
unsigned long and re-casting to obj_cgroup pointer away from the user.

I was just trying to skip one cast, but I think it won't matter too much
anyways since it's always hidden away. I'll change it to obj_cgroup **
in the next version!!

I hope you have a great day : -)
Joshua

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested changing the objcg parameter from a boolean to a pool parameter, allowing for more flexibility and reducing churn in code paths that handle zram and zswap objects differently.
reviews.raw_body:
Instead of passing in a boolean here, what if we make it a pool
parameter at creation time? I don't foresee I use case where some
objects are charged and some aren't. This avoids needing to always
pass objcg=true (for zswap) or objcg=false (for zram), and reduces
churn. Also, it allows us to add assertions to zs_obj_write() (and
elsewhere if needed) that an objcg is passed in when the pool should
be charged.

We can even add a zs_obj_write_objcg() variant that takes in an objcg,
and keep the current one as-is. Both would internally call a helper
that takes in an objcg, but that would further minimize churn to zram.
Not sure if that's worth it though. Sergey, WDYT?


On Thu, Feb 26, 2026 at 11:29 AM Joshua Hahn <joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com> wrote:

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary: Author agreed that modifying zpool's ABI signature is unnecessary, plans to leave it intact.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Yosry,

Thank you for another great idea! Makes sense to me, then I can leave most
of the existing ABI signatures alone and just make the zpool changes.

Thanks a lot again : -)
Joshua

2026-02-26.patch_summary: This patch introduces an array of obj_cgroup pointers to the zpdesc structure in the Linux kernel's zsmalloc module. The purpose is to track the memory group ownership (memcg) of compressed objects. This change increases the size of struct zpdesc by 8 bytes, but since it overlays a page structure, this increase remains invisible outside. However, functions that check for memcg data may be affected and need to use a specific bit to tag the pointer correctly. The patch also temporarily increases memory usage in zswap due to duplicated pointers, but future patches will redirect charging operations and remove the extra pointer. This change is guarded under CONFIG_MEMCG and can optionally be limited to CONFIG_MEMCG && CONFIG_ZSWAP for minimal impact on zram users.
